title: Add upstream buildahimage
body:
Add an upstream buildahimage, built like the git variant, but
remove the /root/buildah directory and packages required for
building but not running buildah.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: What is the difference between buildahimageupstream and buildahimagegit?

@vrothberg the initial imagebuildahgit is 1.32 GB, the one before I added your dnf clean was about 760 MB, now with this change it's down to 486 MB.  The stable bulidah image is 332 MB, that just does an install of buildah latest.
Couldn't restart the one flakey test, so I've repushed, hopefully it will go through this time.
There it goes, all happy green test buttons.
Given @rhatdan already LGTMed I will go ahead an merge :) Thanks for the great work! Really cool stuff :)

:pushpin: Commit d972c76 has been approved by `vrothberg`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=vrothberg d972c76b506df41153315ea5725d5bb3c3822ca4 -->
:hourglass: Testing commit d972c76 with merge bf8f7a0...
FYI @vbatts.  Not sure if you'll need to adjust due to this, but I'm planning on renameing the current buildahimagegit to buildahimageupstream.  Holler if that causes indigestion.

changes seem fine, but the name change seems not ideal.
Since there are no file path changes this does not break our build of the images.
